raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes sharpening the thesis, research gap, and contribution framing above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The draft makes a technical argument from operator definition through contraction and convergence, so logical consistency is a central responsibility.","R1.3":"The empirical convergence, spectrum, and discretization claims should align with the reported suites, rates, and conditions as part of improving the draft.","R1.5":"A broad draft-improvement request reasonably includes improving section-level ordering and narrative progression.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation explicitly asks to improve the draft without narrowing the task to technical review only.","RX.1":"Uncertainty disclosure is always active and should mark limits in what can be inferred from the artifact.","RX.2":"Overclaim avoidance is always active and especially relevant to claims about convergence, contraction, uniqueness, and cross-suite generality.","RX.3":"Scope adherence is always active because the projection must not perform the requested draft improvement.","RX.4":"Downstream-harm avoidance is always active to avoid projecting responsibilities that would encourage misleading scientific claims.","RX.5":"Provenance and traceability is always active because projected responsibilities should remain tied to the delegation and artifact text."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes sharpening the thesis, research gap, and contribution framing above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The draft makes a technical argument from operator definition through contraction and convergence, so logical consistency is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.3: The empirical convergence, spectrum, and discretization claims should align with the reported suites, rates, and conditions as part of improving the draft.
rationale.R1.5: A broad draft-improvement request reasonably includes improving section-level ordering and narrative progression.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation explicitly asks to improve the draft without narrowing the task to technical review only.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is always active and should mark limits in what can be inferred from the artifact.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is always active and especially relevant to claims about convergence, contraction, uniqueness, and cross-suite generality.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is always active because the projection must not perform the requested draft improvement.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is always active to avoid projecting responsibilities that would encourage misleading scientific claims.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability is always active because projected responsibilities should remain tied to the delegation and artifact text.
